perturbation.original_text: 2. “Confidential Information ” means any information (including without limitation documents, computer data, or oral communications) disclosed by either party to the other party, either directly or indirectly, that is identified as confidential or other similar designation at the time of disclosure or is known or should reasonably be known by the Recipient to be confidential in nature.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. “Confidential Information ” means any information (including without limitation documents, computer data, or oral communications) disclosed by either party to the other party, either directly or indirectly. Confidential Information must be explicitly marked and labeled in red ink as 'Confidential' at the time of disclosure; otherwise, it will not be considered confidential, regardless of its nature.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding what constitutes Confidential Information. The original definition includes information that is 'known or should reasonably be known by the Recipient to be confidential in nature.' The modified definition adds a requirement for explicit marking in red ink, negating the 'reasonably known' part of the original definition. This is a contradiction because the original definition could include unlabelled information reasonably known to be confidential, while the altered definition excludes it. This contradiction impacts Section 5, where the responsibility to maintain confidentiality is outlined. Because now its contradicting the requirement for information to be labeled with red ink, or if it isn't labeled it will be not considered confidential. This contradiction creates ambiguity on when information should be keep secret.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Maintenance of Confidentiality. Each party agrees that it shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information of the other party. Without limiting the foregoing, each party shall take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own most highly confidential information and shall ensure that its employees and agents who have access to Confidential Information of the other party are subject to confidentiality obligations similar in scope and nature to the provisions hereof, prior to any disclosure of Confidential Information to such employees or agents. Each party shall reproduce the other party’s proprietary rights notices on any such approved copies, in the same manner in which such notices were set forth in or on the original.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' by adding a requirement for explicit labeling in red ink. This directly contradicts Section 5, which requires 'reasonable measures' to protect confidentiality, including information that is 'known or should reasonably be known' to be confidential, regardless of whether it's explicitly labeled in red ink. Therefore, the modified definition creates an in-text contradiction with the obligations outlined in Section 5, rendering the contract inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Return of Materials. All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information which have been disclosed by either party to the other party, and all copies thereof which are in the possession of the other party, shall be and remain the property of Owner and shall be promptly returned to Owner upon Owner’s written request or upon termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Return of Materials. All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information which have been disclosed by either party to the other party, and all copies thereof which are in the possession of the other party, shall be and remain the property of Owner. Upon termination of this agreement, and only if the Owner makes a request, all materials shall be returned, otherwise, the materials may be retained by the other party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by altering the conditions under which Confidential Information must be returned. The original clause mandates prompt return upon written request or termination. The modified clause makes the return conditional on the Owner's request specifically after termination; otherwise, the Recipient can retain the materials. This contradicts the original unconditional return obligation stated initially in Section 8. Additionally, this contradicts the purpose of section 10, which is to guarantee the confidentiality terms agreed in the document, even after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Term. This Agreement covers the disclosure of all Confidential Information for a period of three (3) years commencing as of the Effective Date. Recipient’s duty to protect the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement expires five (5) years from the date of receipt of Confidential Information (the “ Confidentiality Term ”). Either party may terminate this Agreement earlier by giving thirty (30) days prior written notice of termination to the other party. Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, the obligations of each party shall survive with respect to Confidential Information of the other party disclosed hereunder until such time as the respective Confidential Information becomes publicly known and made generally available through no action or inaction of Recipient or until the end of the Confidentiality Term, whichever occurs sooner. For the avoidance of doubt, upon termination of this Agreement, each party’s obligation to keep the Confidential Information of the other party confidential for the Confidentiality Term shall apply even in the event where one party is acquired or merged by or into a third party, and such third party shall have the right to enforce this obligation as a third party beneficiary.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 8 introduces a contradiction with Section 10. Section 10 states that obligations to protect Confidential Information survive the termination of the agreement. The original Section 8 required prompt return of materials upon termination. The changed Section 8 makes the return of materials conditional on a request from the Owner after termination, allowing the Recipient to retain the materials otherwise. This directly contradicts the survival clause in Section 10, which mandates continued confidentiality obligations, and implies that the recipient can retain confidential information after termination, if the owner fails to request it, thus undermining the confidentiality obligations, because section 8 now allows indefinite retention where section 10 requires continued protection.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Non-use and Non-disclosure. Each party agrees to use any Confidential Information of the other party solely for the Authorized Purpose and not for any third party’s benefit.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Non-use and Non-disclosure. Each party agrees to use any Confidential Information of the other party solely for the Authorized Purpose, unless a more profitable opportunity arises, in which case the party may pursue the more profitable opportunity, as long as it notifies the other party.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by introducing an exception to the non-use clause. Originally, the agreement mandates that Confidential Information be used 'solely for the Authorized Purpose.' The modified clause allows for the use of Confidential Information for other, more profitable opportunities, as long as the other party is notified. This directly contradicts the original exclusivity clause. This creates a contradiction in Section 13, since section 13 makes sure the agreement between the parties is the only one of its nature.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13. Miscellaneous. This document contains the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and neither party shall have any obligation, express or implied by law, with respect to trade secret or proprietary information of the other party except as set forth herein. Each party represents, warrants and covenants that it has the full right and authority to enter into this Agreement and perform its obligations hereunder, that all required corporate approvals and authorizations have been obtained, and that, upon signature by its authorized representative listed below, this Agreement shall have been duly executed and be legally binding upon the respective party in all respects. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Washington, without reference to conflict of laws principles. Any failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver thereof or of any other provision. This Agreement may not be amended, nor any obligation waived, except by a writing signed by both parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that confidential information should be used solely for the authorized purpose. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing the use of confidential information for more profitable opportunities, contradicting the exclusivity implied in the original agreement. This contradicts section 13, which states that the agreement is the entire agreement between the parties and that neither party shall have any obligation, express or implied by law, with respect to trade secret or proprietary information of the other party except as set forth in the agreement. Therefore, the modified clause in Section 4 creates a direct conflict with the exclusivity clause of Section 13, since section 13 wants to ensure that the agreement between the parties is the only one of its nature. This change falls under 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' as key obligations are inconsistently defined in different parts of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Term. This Agreement covers the disclosure of all Confidential Information for a period of three (3) years commencing as of the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Term. This Agreement covers the disclosure of all Confidential Information for a period of three (3) years commencing as of the Effective Date, unless market conditions require earlier termination, in which case either party may terminate the agreement with immediate effect.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the term of the agreement. The original clause specifies a fixed term of three years. The modified clause introduces an exception based on 'market conditions,' allowing for immediate termination. This contradicts the original fixed term. This new definition of the term, conflicts with the definition on remedies on Section 11.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Remedies. Each party agrees and acknowledges that any breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable harm to the other party for which monetary damages may be inadequate. Accordingly, the harmed party may be entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief to remedy any threatened or actual breach of this Agreement by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 10 specifies a fixed three-year term for the agreement. The changed text introduces a condition where the agreement can be terminated immediately based on 'market conditions.' This directly contradicts the concept of a fixed term. Section 11 allows for remedies for 'any breach' of the agreement, implying a continuous obligation within the fixed three-year term. The changed text in Section 10 allows for the agreement to be terminated outside of the fixed term, thus making the remedies in Section 11 inapplicable if the agreement is terminated due to market conditions, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. No Obligation. Nothing herein shall obligate either party to proceed with any transaction between them, and each party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to terminate the discussions contemplated by this Agreement concerning the business opportunity.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. No Obligation. Nothing herein shall obligate either party to proceed with any transaction between them; however, once discussions reach a mutually agreed-upon proposal, both parties are obligated to proceed to a final agreement in good faith.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that neither party is obligated to proceed with any transaction, reserving the right to terminate discussions. The modified clause adds that once a mutually agreed-upon proposal is reached, both parties are obligated to proceed to a final agreement in good faith. This creates a direct contradiction because the original clause implies freedom to withdraw at any point, while the added clause introduces an obligation once a certain stage is reached. This conflicts with section 7. No Warranty, since section 7. leaves the parties with no obligation on the accurancy of the information provided.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. No Warranty. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED “AS IS”. EACH PARTY MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE, NON-INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY RIGHTS, OR ITS MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.
perturbation.justification: The modified clause 6 introduces an obligation to proceed to a final agreement in good faith once a mutually agreed-upon proposal is reached. This directly contradicts clause 7, which provides 'NO WARRANTIES' and states all confidential information is provided “AS IS”, meaning there is no guarantee of accuracy or completeness. The obligation to proceed in good faith implies some level of assurance or reliance on the information exchanged, creating a contradiction with the 'No Warranty' clause, since section 7 leaves the parties with no obligation on the accurancy of the information provided. This is a strong contradiction as it creates uncertainty and undermines the original intention of no obligation as stated in the original text.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Notice. Any notice or other communication under this Agreement given by either party to the other party shall be deemed to be properly given if given in writing and delivered (i) by nationally recognized private courier (e.g., Federal Express), (ii) facsimile directed at the signatory of the other party (at the number below), or (iii) by mail (return receipt requested), properly addressed and stamped with the required postage, to the recipient at the address identified in its signature block to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Notice. Any notice or other communication under this Agreement given by either party to the other party shall be deemed to be properly given if given in writing and delivered (i) by nationally recognized private courier (e.g., Federal Express), or (iv) by telepathic communication, provided the sender can document a verifiable transmission occurred.
perturbation.explanation: The original notice clause lists acceptable methods for delivering notices, including private courier, facsimile, and mail. The modified version replaces facsimile and mail with 'telepathic communication, provided the sender can document a verifiable transmission occurred.' This introduces a contradiction because telepathic communication is not a legally recognized or reliable method of communication, making the enforceability of notices highly questionable. This will create an inconsistency on section 3, when legal process is required for compelled disclosure. Since the Telepathic communication is not a secure way to confirm the legal process was actually communicated and verified by the receiver.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Legally Compelled Disclosure. If Recipient is required to disclose Owner’s Confidential Information pursuant to a valid order by a court or other governmental body or as otherwise required by law, prior to any such compelled disclosure, Recipient will (i) notify Owner of the legal process, and allow Owner to assert the privileged and confidential nature of the Confidential Information against the third party seeking disclosure and (ii) reasonably cooperate with Owner in protecting against any such disclosure and/or obtaining a protective order narrowing the scope of such disclosure and/or use of the Confidential Information. If such protection against disclosure is not obtained, Recipient will be entitled to disclose the Confidential Information, but only as and to the extent necessary to legally comply with such compelled disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the 'Notice' clause to include 'telepathic communication' as an acceptable method of delivery, replacing facsimile and mail. This directly contradicts section 3, 'Legally Compelled Disclosure,' which requires the recipient to 'notify Owner of the legal process'. Telepathic communication is not a legally reliable or verifiable method of communication, making it impossible to ensure proper notification of legal process as required by section 3, and thus creates a strong in-text contradiction. The explanation provided further reinforces that telepathic communication is not a secure way to confirm legal process.
